-# Mycelium : For self-improving codebases +# Mycelium -### 116 pages. 62 JS modules. 30 API routes. 3 languages. Zero frameworks. - -A browser-based world built entirely with **vanilla JS**, **Hono**, and **Cloudflare Workers**.
-No React. No Vue. No Next. Just HTML files and a lot of stubbornness. - -[![Live Site](https://img.shields.io/badge/Live-numbahwan.pages.dev-ff9500?style=for-the-badge&logo=cloudflare&logoColor=white)](https://numbahwan.pages.dev) -[![The Oracle](https://img.shields.io/badge/Try-The%20Oracle-ffd700?style=for-the-badge&logo=sparkles&logoColor=black)](https://numbahwan.pages.dev/oracle) -[![Showcase](https://img.shields.io/badge/Browse-All%20116%20Pages-333?style=for-the-badge)](https://numbahwan.pages.dev/showcase) +### Self-improving codebase immune system -
+**Four scripts. Zero dependencies. Drop into any repo.**
+Learns from every commit, warns before repeating mistakes, auto-fixes with cryptographic proof. -[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE) -[![Pages](https://img.shields.io/badge/HTML%20pages-116-ff6b00)](https://numbahwan.pages.dev/showcase) -[![JS Modules](https://img.shields.io/badge/JS%20modules-62-ffd700)](https://numbahwan.pages.dev/showcase) -[![API Routes](https://img.shields.io/badge/API%20routes-30-00d4ff)]() -[![i18n](https://img.shields.io/badge/languages-EN%20%7C%20繁中%20%7C%20ไทย-28a745)]() -[![Zero Frameworks](https://img.shields.io/badge/frameworks-zero-e8e0d8)]() +[![Mycelium Score](https://img.shields.io/badge/Mycelium_Score-75%2F100_(B)-6c5ce7?style=for-the-badge)](https://numbahwan.pages.dev/showcase) +[![Tests](https://img.shields.io/badge/Regression_Tests-33_passing-00b894?style=for-the-badge)]() +[![Zero Dependencies](https://img.shields.io/badge/Dependencies-Zero-e8e0d8?style=for-the-badge)]()
--- -## Mycelium — Codebase Immune System +## What Is Mycelium? -**The world's first self-improving codebase.** Four scripts that learn from every commit, remember every breakage, diagnose root causes, prescribe fixes, and prove improvement with cryptographic evidence. - -> Like biological mycelium — the underground fungal network that connects trees, shares nutrients, and strengthens the entire forest — this system connects your files, shares constraints, and strengthens your codebase with every commit. +Like biological mycelium — the underground fungal network that connects trees and strengthens the entire forest — this system connects your files, shares constraints, and strengthens your codebase with every commit. ```bash -# Unified CLI -npx mycelium help # See all commands -npx mycelium eval # 9 KPIs with cryptographic proof -npx mycelium diagnose # Root-cause analysis → files → failure modes -npx mycelium fix # Auto-diagnose → prescribe → execute → verify -npx mycelium status # Current state + pending prescriptions -npx mycelium health # Project health summary -npx mycelium version # Component status + data sizes +# Add to any project in 30 seconds +cp mycelium.cjs mycelium-watch.cjs mycelium-eval.cjs mycelium-fix.cjs your-project/ +cd your-project && node mycelium.cjs --init +# Done. It learns from your git history immediately. ``` ### The Four Components | Script | Role | What It Does | |--------|------|-------------| -| `mycelium.cjs` | **The Learner** | Snapshots every commit, tracks hotspots, detects couplings, stores constraints in `.mycelium/memory.json` | -| `mycelium-watch.cjs` | **The Watcher** | Analyzes commits, scores risk, identifies repeat offenders, maps file relationships in `.mycelium/watch.json` | -| `mycelium-eval.cjs` | **The Evaluator** | Single source of truth: 9 weighted KPIs, honest before/after split, SHA-256 cryptographic proof, 25 self-checks | +| `mycelium.cjs` | **The Learner** | Snapshots every commit, tracks hotspots, detects couplings, stores constraints | +| `mycelium-watch.cjs` | **The Watcher** | Analyzes git history, scores file risk, warns before repeating mistakes | +| `mycelium-eval.cjs` | **The Evaluator** | 9 weighted KPIs, honest before/after split, SHA-256 cryptographic proof, 28 self-checks | | `mycelium-fix.cjs` | **The Fixer** | Diagnoses friction, prescribes 6 fix types, executes automatically, verifies improvement | ### How It Works ``` Every commit triggers: - watch --learn → snapshot → eval → diagnose → fix (if needed) + watch --learn -> snapshot -> eval -> diagnose -> fix (if needed) Pre-commit hook: - watch --warn → guard constraints → BLOCK if violated + watch --warn -> guard constraints -> BLOCK if violated -Pipeline: Learner → Evaluator → Fixer (continuous loop) +Pipeline: Learner -> Evaluator -> Fixer (continuous loop) ``` -
-See the evaluation scorecard — 9 KPIs, weighted scoring, cryptographic proof +### Quick Start -``` -Mycelium-Eval v2.0 — Foolproof Learning System Evaluation +```bash +# Install (zero dependencies, just copy files) +node mycelium.cjs --init - Overall: 69/100 (C) [proof: 60a5319cca03a01f] +# See what it learned +node mycelium.cjs --status +node mycelium.cjs --onboard - Scorecard: - ████████░░ Fix Rate Trend 50/100 (15%) real bug fix rate flat - █░░░░░░░░░ Repeat Prevention 10/100 (15%) majority repeat 53% - ██████████ Constraint Coverage 100/100 (15%) 6/6 areas covered - ██████████ Lesson Quality 100/100 (15%) 50/50 lessons specific - ██░░░░░░░░ Fix Chain Speed 20/100 (10%) chains growing 0→4 - ██████████ Knowledge Density 100/100 (10%) 141/141 files - █████████░ Warning Coverage 90/100 (10%) 63% warning rate - ██████████ Bundle Health 100/100 (5%) 390KB < 400KB - ████████░░ Fixer Effectiveness 80/100 (5%) 8 fixes, guard enforced - - Verification: 25/25 self-checks passed - Proof: SHA-256 hash 60a5319cca03a01f — re-run to verify -``` - -

-See the diagnosis engine — root causes mapped to specific files +# Evaluate your codebase learning health +node mycelium-eval.cjs +# Auto-fix weak areas +node mycelium-fix.cjs --force ``` -Friction Analysis — Root-Cause Diagnosis - - Fix Rate Trend: 50/100 (weight 15%) - Root causes: 5 files (cards.html 10x, markets.html 7x, battle.html 7x...) - Prescription: PREVENT_NEW_BUGS → add guards to top 5 offenders - Expected: +15-30 points - Repeat Prevention: 10/100 (weight 15%) - Root causes: 16/30 files repeat (53%) - Prescriptions: - STRENGTHEN_CONSTRAINTS → 7 files (+27 preventive rules) - ADD_CONSTRAINTS → 8 files (+24 constraints from lessons) - ENFORCE_CO_CHANGES → 3 coupled pairs (battle↔engine, markets↔index) - Expected: +14 points - - Fix Chain Speed: 20/100 (weight 10%) - Root causes: 3 chains (4-fix UI chain, 2-fix coupling chain, 6-fix mobile chain) - Prescriptions: - PRE_MORTEM_FOR_CHAIN_FILES → require pre-mortem before editing - ROOT_CAUSE_FIRST → every fix must include root-cause note - Expected: +8 points +### CLI Reference - Summary: 3 friction points, 6 prescriptions, +30 pts potential +```bash +# Unified CLI +npx mycelium help # See all commands +npx mycelium eval # 9 KPIs with cryptographic proof +npx mycelium diagnose # Root-cause analysis -> files -> failure modes +npx mycelium fix # Auto-diagnose -> prescribe -> execute -> verify +npx mycelium status # Current state + pending prescriptions +npx mycelium ship "msg" # Atomic deploy: commit -> test -> sync -> PR -> merge ``` -

 # CLAUDE.md — Mycelium Session Protocol
 
 ## !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
-## !! STOP. READ .nw-context BEFORE YOU WRITE A SINGLE LINE OF CODE.
+## !! STOP. READ .mycelium-context BEFORE YOU WRITE A SINGLE LINE OF CODE.

-## !! RUN: cat .nw-context
+## !! RUN: cat .mycelium-context
 ## !! IF YOU DON'T, THE PRE-COMMIT HOOK WILL BLOCK YOUR COMMIT.
 ## !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
 
 ## Session Startup Checklist (MANDATORY — do ALL of these FIRST)
 ```bash
 # 1. Read the brain — this has rules, breakages, decisions, fragile files
-cat .nw-context
+cat .mycelium-context
 
 # 2. Create session marker (REQUIRED or commits will be blocked)
-echo $(date +%s) > .nw-session
+echo $(date +%s) > .mycelium-session
 
 # 3. Pre-mortem for the area you're about to touch
 node mycelium.cjs --premortem <area>
 ```
 
 If you skip steps 1-2, the pre-commit hook will reject your commit with:
-`BLOCKED: No .nw-session file. Read CLAUDE.md and run the startup checklist.`
+`BLOCKED: No .mycelium-session file. Read CLAUDE.md and run the startup checklist.`
 
 ## CRITICAL: Mobile-First iOS Development
 
@@ -74,9 +74,9 @@ This isn't optional. Every session produces knowledge. Record it.
 
 ## The Protocol
 ```
-1. cat .nw-context              ← read the brain (includes WIP if any)
-2. echo

+## CRITICAL: Deployment — ALWAYS use `mycelium ship`
+**NEVER** manually run `git push` + `gh pr create` + `gh pr merge` separately.
+Two bugs recurred 3+ times before this was fixed:
+1. **Auth token expires** → `git push` fails with "Invalid username or token"
+2. **Workflow stops after PR creation** → PR never gets merged
+
+The fix is `mycelium ship` which does all 9 steps atomically:
+```bash
+node bin/mycelium.cjs ship "feat: my change description"
+```
+Steps: [1] auto-commit → [2] validate (eval+tests) → [3] refresh auth → [4] sync remote → [5] squash → [6] push → [7] PR → [8] merge → [9] sync local
+
 ## CRITICAL: Chat Compaction Survival
 Chat platforms compress history, which **destroys in-flight task state** (todo lists, progress).
 The fix: write your current task to disk with `--wip` BEFORE starting work.

fixCommits.body:
PROBLEM: selfHeal gated at 10 commits, but squash workflow compresses 4-5→1,
so heal almost never fires. Post-commit only printed a reminder instead of acting.
gitwise had no deep root-cause analysis (eval says 'not deep enough').
Both systems were blind to each other's data.

FIXES:
1. selfHeal gate: 10→3 commits (both gitwise + nw-memory)
2. Post-commit hook: auto-runs --heal + --postfix on fix detection (was: just a reminder)
3. Post-merge hook: --sync for both systems (catches PR merges)
4. gitwise Action 7: deep root-cause analysis — extracts recurring themes from
   repeat offenders (word frequency across breakage lessons)
5. gitwise Action 8: cross-system sync — maps NW-Memory constraints (by area)
   to gitwise risk files (by path keyword matching). 109 constraints synced.
6. gitwise warn(): now surfaces constraints + deep analysis themes at pre-commit
7. Two new learnings recorded for automation area

PIPELINE NOW: commit → hook → learn → snapshot → selfHeal(3) → eval → sync
N

fixCommits.body:
NW-Memory self-healing architecture:
- runNwEval(mem): pure scoring function, returns data (7 metrics, A-F grade)
- selfHealNw(mem): auto-fix weak scores (6 corrective actions)
- Wired into takeSnapshot(): runs silently every 10 snapshots
- --heal CLI command: force immediate eval + fix cycle
- --eval now also triggers self-heal after printing results

Self-heal actions:
1. Auto-creates constraints from breakage lessons (coverage < 60%)
2. Enriches constraints with gitwise deep lessons (effectiveness < 40%)
3. Promotes breakages to learnings (capture < 60%)
4. Triggers deep reflection (quality < 50%)
5. Marks fix-chain hotspot areas (trend < 50%)
6. Auto-installs gitwise if missing

Current scores: NW-Memory 63/100 (C), gitwise 49/100 (D), combined 56/100 (D)
Build: 549KB | Regression: 13/13 pass

+| gitwise dashboard (danger scores, risks) | `node gitwise.cjs --status` |
+| gitwise warn on staged files | `node gitwise.cjs --warn` |
+| gitwise reinstall (rescan history) | `node gitwise.cjs --install` |
+
+## Two Learning Systems (both active)
+
+### gitwise (passive — zero config, zero commands)
+gitwise watches every commit and warns before you repeat a mistake. You don't run it.
+- **Post-commit hook**: auto-learns breakages, file couplings, risk patterns
+- **Pre-commit hook**: auto-warns if staged files have broken before or are missing coupled files
+- **Dashboard**: `node gitwise.cjs --status` (danger scores, riskiest files, couplings)
+- **Manual warn**: `node gitwise.cjs --warn` (test what warnings staged files would get)
+- **Install on any repo**: `node gitwise.cjs --install` (scans history, sets up hooks)
+
+### NW-Memory (active — discipline-based power tool)
+NW-Memory stores constraints, decisions, breakages, and learnings you explicitly record.
+Both tools share intelligence: NW-Memory uses gitwise's deep lesson extraction (commit body + diff analysis)
+for auto-reflect, postfix, and breakage recording.
+
+| What | gitwise does automatically | NW-Memory adds on top |
+|------|--------------------------|----------------------|
+| Learn from fixes | Reads commit body + diff | Stores in constraints/learnings |
+| Warn before mistakes | Pre-commit: "broke 7x, danger 31.9" | --guard: area constraints + coupled files |
+| Risk tracking | Danger score per file | Area-level breakage history |
+| Lessons | 98% specific from diffs | Human-curated + auto-extracted |
+| Workflow | Invisible | Session protocol + WIP persistence |
 
 ## Areas
 battle, forge, i18n, nav, economy, collection, wallet, cards, tabletop, emoji, dom, ios, modules, font, memory, workflow, oracle, sentinel, lore, discoverability, absurd, exchange

fixCommits.msg: fix(battle): Don't block init on Audio.init() - fixes iOS hand empty bug
fixCommits.body:
Root cause found via mobile debug: init() was stuck at 'await Audio.init()'
on iOS. Web Audio API requires user gesture on iOS Safari, so Audio.init()
was hanging/slow. This blocked the START button from being wired, meaning
deck building and hand dealing never happened.

Fix: Audio.init() now runs in background (fire-and-forget with catch).
START button is wired immediately after cards load. Cache bust v5.2.

fixCommits.msg: fix(battle): Move deck building + hand dealing to START button click
fixCommits.body:
Root cause: Decks were built and hands dealt during init() (page load),
but before the user clicked START. By the time startGame() ran,
gameState could be stale or hands empty due to race conditions.

Fix:
- Moved all deck building and hand dealing INTO the START button handler
- Cards are now dealt fresh RIGHT BEFORE the countdown starts
- Added safety check in startGame() — if hand is still empty, re-deal
- Added debug logging: deck sizes and hand sizes at each step
- renderHand() has null/gameStats safety checks

This ensures cards are ALWAYS in hand when the game renders.

fixCommits.body:
Root cause: .arena-container was position:fixed with no overflow,
and .battle-board had flex:1 which greedily consumed all space,
pushing the hand section and action bar below the viewport on mobile.

Fixes:
- Add overflow-y:auto to .arena-container so content can scroll
- Change .battle-board from flex:1 to flex:1 1 0 with min-height:0
- Add flex-shrink:0 to .hand-section and .action-bar
- Set .battle-board to flex:0 0 auto in mobile breakpoints (768px, 480px)
- Set .board-row to flex:0 0 auto to prevent vertical stretching
- Add safe-area-inset-bottom padding for iOS notch phones
- Add -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch for smooth mobile scrolling

fixCommits.msg: fix: Restore homepage — remove GSAP defer, fix digit-starting i18n keys
fixCommits.body:
Root cause: The defer attribute we added to GSAP CDN scripts broke index.html.
GSAP was loading AFTER inline scripts that depend on it, causing:
- 'gsap is not defined' error
- All .reveal elements stuck at opacity:0 (never animated in)
- CP Race data never loaded
- Hero RegginA portrait invisible (content below stuck hidden)
- Everything appeared dark/empty

Fixes:
1. Removed defer from GSAP scripts on index.html (has 21 inline gsap refs)
2. Removed defer from Chart.js on markets.html (has inline Chart usage)
3. Fixed invalid JS keys starting with digits (9ActiveMembers, 2024NumbahwanGuild,
   1stEdition, 0Days, 5Luck, 7slotWorkGloves, etc.) — JS requires quoted keys
   for digit-starting identifiers. Prefixed all with 'n' across 9 pages.

Results: 0 console errors | 149/149 tests green | All sections rendering

fixCommits.msg: feat(sentinel): Upgrade to v2.5 — 10-module health platform with trend tracking & auto-fix engine
fixCommits.body:
SENTINEL v2.5.0 — Ultimate Code Health Platform

10 Audit Modules (was 6):
  1. Architecture (15%) — file size, complexity, route distribution
  2. Assets (15%) — image optimization, CSS/JS bloat, format audit
  3. i18n (10%) — translation coverage, hardcoded strings
  4. Dead Code (10%) — unused exports, orphan pages
  5. Security (15%) — secrets, unsafe patterns, SQL injection, headers
  6. Performance (10%) — bundle size, deps, page weight, lazy loading
  7. API Surface (8%) — 186 endpoint inventory, error handling, rate limiting
  8. Dependencies (7%) — unused deps, @types misplacement, lock file
  9. Accessibility (5%) — H1/main/alt/ARIA/form labels, semantic HTML
 10. SEO & Meta (5%) — title/description/OG/canonical/sitemap/robots

New Systems:
  - Trend Tracker: scores across builds, regression detection, streaks
  - Auto-Fix Engine: 10 fix scripts (6 automated, 4 manual)
  - Regression Gate: --no-regress CI flag blocks score drops

14 API Endpoints:
  /sentinel, /sentinel/quick

`).join(''); - const earnHtml = c.howToEarn.map(e => `${e}`).join(''); + // Get localized howToGet array, with fallback + const earnArr = c.howToGet ? (c.howToGet[lang] || c.howToGet['en'] || []) : []; + const earnHtml = earnArr.map(e => `${e}`).join(''); return `

fixCommits.body:

- Disabled aurora wave background animations - static transforms
- Made loading rings static (il-ring-1, il-ring-2)
- Disabled emblem pulse and title glow animations
- Made loader dots static instead of bouncing
- Disabled maple leaf filter for GPU efficiency
- Removed orb filter blur for better performance
- Disabled shimmer animation on progress bars
- Made crown icons static with glow instead of float
